# Test bash coverage for scripts in txtar files
env MCP_BASH_COVERAGE=1
env MCP_BASH_COVERAGE_DIR=$WORK/bash-coverage

# Make the script executable
exec chmod +x test.sh

# Run a bash script from txtar file
bash './test.sh hello'

# Check output
stdout 'Starting test'
stdout 'Hello from test.sh'
stdout 'Argument: hello'
stdout 'You said hello!'

# Check trace file was created
exec bash -c 'ls $WORK/bash-coverage/ | grep -E "bash-.*\.trace"'

# Look at trace content
exec bash -c 'cat $WORK/bash-coverage/bash-*.trace | head -5'

-- test.sh --
#!/bin/bash
echo "Starting test"
echo "Hello from test.sh" 
echo "Argument: $1"

if [ "$1" = "hello" ]; then
    echo "You said hello!"
fi
